title: Timothy Zahn
content:
{{Top|rwp}} {{Multiple_issues|cleanup|expand|updatecontent=[[The Long Game (Star Wars Insider 172)|The Long Game]], {{YouTube|video=u-ZRf_-MIN4|text=Mysterious Galaxy Virtual Event: Author Timothy Zahn in conversation with Tom Hoeler|channel=MysteriousGalaxy|name=Mysterious Galaxy}}}} {{Person |image=[[File:Zahn.jpg]] |name=Timothy Zahn |birth=[[September 1]], [[1950s#1951|1951]],{{ThisWeek|ZX8wK78600E|The Mandalorian Season 2 Date Announced, A New Look at The High Republic, and More!}}
Chicago, Illinois{{WebCite|url=http://www.chicagotribune.com/entertainment/movies/ct-timothy-zahn-star-wars-ent-0523-20170522-column.html|text=Novelist Timothy Zahn is the man who saved 'Star Wars,' according to fans|work=[[Wikipedia:Chicago Tribune|Chicago Tribune]]|archivedate=20211218235305}} |death= |listedpronouns= |nationality=[[Wikipedia:America|American]] |occupation=Author |companies= |website= }} {{Quote|It was just after four o'clock on November 6, 1989, and I was three days into writing my first novel for my new publisher, Bantam Books, when the phone rang. It was my agent. 'Tim,' he said after the usual pleasantries, 'we have a very interesting offer here.'|Timothy Zahn|''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]''}} '''Timothy Zahn''' is a science fiction author which is best known for his work on [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|''The Thrawn Trilogy'']], a series of novels following the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']], introducing various characters such as [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] and [[Mara Jade Skywalker]]. In addition to numerous short stories, he also wrote other novels focusing on Thrawn's character, such as [[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] and [[Star Wars: The Hand of Thrawn Duology|''The Hand of Thrawn Duology'']] in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity and the [[Star Wars: Thrawn (novel series)|''Thrawn'']] and [[Star Wars: The Ascendancy Trilogy|''The Ascendancy Trilogy'']] novel series in the current [[canon]] continuity. ==Biography== ===Expanding the universe=== {{Quote|I wandered around the house for the next couple of hours, lost in a fog of excitement, anticipation, and panic, wondering if I could possibly come up with a story that would do justice to George Lucas's vision.|Timothy Zahn|}} Timothy Zahn's first book in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' franchise was ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'', which followed the events after [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|Star Wars: Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']].''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' He later released ''[[Dark Force Rising]]'' as a sequel to the former,''[[Dark Force Rising]]'' and later concluded the trilogy with ''[[The Last Command]]''.''[[The Last Command]]'' He later wrote a [[Star Wars: The Hand of Thrawn Duology|duology]] which consisted of ''[[Specter of the Past]]''''[[Specter of the Past]]'' and ''[[Vision of the Future]]''.''[[Vision of the Future]]'' Zahn also wrote various standalone novels, such as [[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']], which told one of Thrawn's [[Battle for Outbound Flight|first]] victories, which gained him the respect of [[Darth Sidious]].[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] ''[[Scoundrels]]'',''[[Scoundrels]]'' ''[[Survivor's Quest]]'',''[[Survivor's Quest]]'' [[Allegiance (novel)|''Allegiance'']][[Allegiance (novel)|''Allegiance'']] and ''[[Choices of One]]''.''[[Choices of One]]'' He also contributed to the script of several [[Star Wars comic books|comics]] and short stories such as ''[[Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand]]''.''[[Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand]]'' [[File:Maracorrantalon.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Talon Karrde]] (portrayed by Zahn, right) with [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] (portrayed by [[Shannon McRandle]]) and [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] (portrayed by [[Michael A. Stackpole|Michael Stackpole]]).]] Zahn later portrayed [[Talon Karrde]] from ''The Thrawn Trilogy'', for the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]].{{CCG|set=Reflections II: Expanding the Galaxy|cardname=Talon Karrde}} As Karrde had much more hair than his creator, Zahn needed to use hair extensions. He was also involved in the writing of Copero flashpoint in ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''.{{TORweb|url=info/news/news-article/20171017-0|text=First Look: 'Traitor Among The Chiss' Teaser}} ===Planned works=== Before Zahn was hired to write a ninth ''Star Wars'' novel, Zahn had planned a number of ideas including stories of the [[Skywalker family/Legends|Skywalker family]], more adventures of the [[Hand of Judgment]] and more of Thrawn's and [[Mara Jade Skywalker]]'s backstories.{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/jedicouncil/interview/timothyzahn.asp|text=Jedi Council Interviews - Timothy Zahn|work=[[TheForce.net]]|archivedate=20200602130156}} At [[Celebration III]], Zahn said he would like to write a novel set after [[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]], featuring [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Mara Jade Skywalker]], and a teenager [[Ben Skywalker|Ben]]. But the possibility of that novel in that time period vanished when Mara died in ''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''. However in a later interview with [[TheForce.net]]. Zahn said he still wanted to do a novel about the Skywalker family with Mara, Ben and Luke or to tell more tales. But it would have had to be before Star Wars: Legacy of the Force. Zahn answered a fan's question at [[Celebration III]], he shared that he would be interested in working more with Thrawn if it involved a new threat that only a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] of the grand admiral could solve. The clone would become unsure of himself due to the people expecting him to be as good as the original Chiss. Zahn stated at a book signing in Philadelphia that he would like the clone of Thrawn to team up with Ben Skywalker.{{JCFCite|url=posts/26116707|board=Literature|thread=Meeting Tim Zahn-Questions|user=Coonsan|date=2007-02-05|time=12:20 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20170329231938/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/meeting-tim-zahn-questions.26100390}} Zahn stated through a Facebook Q&A in [[2013]], that he had a trilogy project to share with [[Lucasfilm]], with one book featuring Thrawn and the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss species]], the second involved Jade preventing an assassination attempt on [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], and the third book would be the third book in the ''Hand of Judgment'' series. The proposed title for the trilogy was ''The Maestro Trilogy''.{{Facebook|username=starwarsbooks|url=starwarsbooks/posts/10151371293428713|name=Star Wars Books|date=2013-01-23|quote=(1) If there are to be more books, and if LFL/Del Rey/Disney decide to let me write some of them, I have a trilogy project to pitch where the third book would also be the third book of the Hand of Judgment series (Allegiance and Choices of One). Again, I don't know if that will happen. (2) A Fel or Chiss novel would be fun. As a matter of fact, the trilogy I mentioned above (which I'll pitch if I'm invited to do another book) would have one book heavily involving Thrawn and the Chiss. (The middle book, btw, would feature Mara being sent to stop an assassination...against Vader. Imagine the fun they would *both* have with *that* assignment.) The proposed series title is The Maestro Trilogy, if anyone wants to star nagging Del Rey. (No, seriously, *don't* nag. Just ask politely...)|archiveurl=https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/File:Timothy_Zahn_Facebook_Q%26A.png}} ===Rebuilding Thrawn and the Chiss=== [[File:Thrawn_Ascendancy_Chaos_Rising_Cover.jpg|250x250px|thumb|left|Timothy Zahn explored the Chiss Ascendancy on a new trilogy]] Followed by the development of the [[Sequel trilogy]] and acquisition of ''Star Wars'' by the Walt Disney Company, in [[April 25]], [[2014]] Lucasfilm established the six original ''Star Wars'' films and the animated ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars|Clone Wars]]'' TV show as [[Canon]], rebranding all Expanded Universe material, including of Zahn's works as ''Legends''.{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary ''Star Wars'' Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}} Zahn hadn't been involved in ''Star Wars'', however, as Thrawn was reintroduced into the saga through ''[[Star Wars Rebels Season Three]]'', through the episode [[Star Wars Rebels: Steps Into Shadow]]{{SWYouTube|0EI8YxV0u2U|Star Wars Celebration Europe 2016 Live Stream – Day 2 | The Star Wars Show LIVE!}} created by [[Dave Filoni]]. He was chosen to write [[Thrawn (novel)|''Thrawn'']], a novel which focused on [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo]], [[Core name|Thrawn]]{{'s}} rise through the [[Galactic Empire]]'s ranks. From [[Lieutenant Commander]] to [[Grand Admiral]]. Which released on [[April 11]], [[2017]].{{SW|url=news/timothy-zahn-star-wars-thrawn-novel-interview|text=Timothy Zahn on His Novel ''Thrawn'', How to Pronounce the Grand Admiral's Full Name, and More}} The novel received a follow up, ''[[Thrawn: Alliances]]'', which told the story of how [[Anakin Skywalker]] and the Chiss defeated the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems|Separatists]] on [[Mokivj]] and how Darth Vader and the Grand Admiral discovered a major threat for the Empire, the [[Grysk Hegemony]].''[[Thrawn: Alliances]]'' It was released on [[June 26]], [[2018]].{{Penguin|url=books/572675/thrawn-alliances-star-wars-by-timothy-zahn|text=Thrawn: Alliances (Star Wars)}} He later released ''[[Thrawn: Treason]]'', the final installment in the trilogy, where Thrawn defeated the Grysk Hegemony with the help of [[Admiral]] [[Ar'alani]], and where he defeated [[Balanhai Savit]].''[[Thrawn: Treason]]'' It was released on [[July 23]], [[2019]]{{Penguin|url=books/606988/thrawn-treason-star-wars-by-timothy-zahn/9781984820983|text=Thrawn: Treason (Star Wars)}} On [[September 1]], [[2020]], Zahn released ''[[Thrawn Ascendancy: Chaos Rising]]'' the first book in the [[Star Wars: The Ascendancy Trilogy]] which was about Thrawn when he was a member of the [[Chiss Ascendancy]].{{Penguin|url=books/622990/star-wars-thrawn-ascendancy-book-i-chaos-rising-by-timothy-zahn/9780593157688|text=Star Wars: Thrawn Ascendancy (Book I: Chaos Rising)}} On [[April 27]], [[2021]], ''[[Thrawn Ascendancy: Greater Good]]'' was released. Being the second book in the ''Ascendancy Trilogy''.{{SW|url=news/thrawn-ascendancy-greater-good-exclusive-excerpt|text=The Chiss Fleet Attacks in ''Thrawn Ascendancy: Greater Good'' – Exclusive Excerpt}} Later, on [[November 16]], [[2021]], ''[[Thrawn Ascendancy: Lesser Evil]]'' was released, which ended the ''Ascendancy Trilogy''.{{SW|url=news/star-wars-thrawn-ascendancy-lesser-evil|text=The ''Star Wars: Ascendancy'' Trilogy Will End With ''Lesser Evil'' – Exclusive Reveal}} For Thrawn's appearance in ''[[Star Wars: Ahsoka|Ahsoka]]'' the [[Disney+]] series, Zahn was regularly consulted by Filoni and other creatives{{Twitter|username=empiremagazine|url=status/1644660148198055940|name=Empire Magazine|quote=Dave Filoni says they have spoken – and are continuing to speak – with Timothy Zahn about Thrawn's character. “That all starts with Tim Zahn's books,” he says. “When we were all kids, hungry for something else, and Heir To The Empire hit,” he remembers, “it was so unique and so wonderful.” Thrawn is not to be messed with. “He doesn't have the Force, but it doesn't matter. He will outsmart you,” says Filoni. #StarWarsCelebration|archivedate=20230408124058}} to ensure the character was written perfectly.{{Twitter|username=sw_holocron|url=status/1644658007383461894|name=Star Wars Holocron|quote=Dave Filoni consulted with Timothy Zahn on how to bring Grand Admiral Thrawn to life in the Ahsoka series. “We want to make sure we get it right.” #StarWars #StarWarsCelebration #StarWarsCelebration2023|archivedate=20230408122554}} ==Works== ===Bibliography=== {| {{Prettytable}}
